steps.title: Spot the logical prediction
steps.content: The first sentence establishes that natural selection was “an obvious next step” given the scientific ideas of the 1800s. This sets an expectation: if a theory is obvious or predictable, independent discoverers should naturally reach it.

steps.title: Connect the consequence
steps.content:
The word 'then' captures the causal link perfectly: it expresses consequence, so Darwin and Wallace's independent discovery becomes the expected outcome—no coincidence.

The other choices either signal contradiction ('however'), break the causal chain ('moreover'), or treat their discovery as an illustration rather than a predicted outcome ('for example').

This matches choice B — B is correct.
